name: Nintendo Entertainment System
description: The console that saved the video game industry after the 1983 crash. Changed gaming forever.

name: Super Nintendo Entertainment System
description: 16-bit powerhouse with Mode 7 graphics. Home to some of the greatest games ever made.

name: Sega Genesis Model 1
description: Blast Processing! The Genesis brought arcade-quality games home. Rivals the SNES.

name: Sega Genesis Model 2
description: Smaller and lighter revision of the Genesis. Same great library at a lower price.

name: Nintendo 64
description: The N64 introduced 3D gaming to a generation. Four controller ports for multiplayer.

name: Original PlayStation
description: Sony's entry into gaming changed everything. CD-based games and 3D graphics.

name: Sega Saturn
description: The Saturn had incredible 2D sprite performance. Underrated but beloved by collectors.

name: Atari 2600
description: The granddaddy of home gaming. Where it all began for millions of gamers.

name: Game Boy Original
description: The grey brick that conquered portable gaming. Nearly indestructible.

name: Game Boy Color
description: Added color to the Game Boy lineup while keeping full backward compatibility.

name: Game Boy Advance
description: The GBA packed SNES-level power into a portable. Incredible library of games.

name: Sega Game Gear
description: Sega's answer to the Game Boy with a full-color backlit screen. Battery hungry!

name: Neo Geo Pocket Color
description: SNK's little gem with a satisfying clicky joystick. Rare and collectable.

name: TurboGrafx-16
description: The forgotten console with a cult following. CD add-on made it a powerhouse.

name: Sega Master System
description: The NES's main competitor outside North America. Great library of its own.

name: PlayStation 2
description: The best-selling console of all time. Incredible library of games and DVD playback built-in.

name: Xbox Original
description: Microsoft's entry into gaming. Built-in hard drive and powerful hardware for the time.

name: Dreamcast
description: Sega's swan song. First console with online gaming built-in. VMU memory cards were revolutionary.

name: GameCube
description: Nintendo's purple cube. Small discs, four controller ports, and iconic games like Melee.

name: Atari 5200
description: The follow-up to the 2600. Better graphics, but the controllers were notoriously difficult.

name: Atari 7800
description: Backward compatible with 2600 games. Jack Tramiel era Atari.

name: ColecoVision
description: Arcade-quality ports of Donkey Kong and others. The system that challenged Atari.

name: Intellivision
description: The 'Intelligent Television' had a unique controller and great sports games.

name: Neo Geo AES
description: The 'Cadillac of consoles' – arcade-perfect games at home, but very expensive at launch.

name: PC Engine
description: The Japanese version of the TurboGrafx-16. Smaller and more popular in Japan.

name: Sega CD
description: Add-on for Genesis that played CD games. Great for FMV titles and enhanced ports.

name: 32X
description: Add-on for Genesis that added 32-bit power. A fascinating but flawed upgrade.

name: Game Boy Advance SP
description: Folding GBA with backlit screen. The ultimate Game Boy experience.

name: Nintendo DS Lite
description: Dual screens, touch screen, and huge library of games. Backward compatible with GBA.

name: PlayStation Portable (PSP)
description: Sony's powerful handheld with UMD discs. Great for media and games.
